source_metadata.title_raw: Elizabeth L. Martyn - A-19-207 - December 11, 2019 - Ontario

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 11, 2019

Elizabeth L. Martyn
Cole Huber LLP
3401 Centrelake Drive, Suite 670
Ontario, CA 91761

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-207

Dear Ms. Martyn:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Rim of the World Park District
Board Member Hugh Bialecki regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act")¹ and Section 1090. Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict
of interest provisions of the Act and Section 1090. We do not provide advice under any other
conflict of interest laws. Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and
accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should
contact us for additional advice.

We are required to forward your request regarding Section 1090 and all pertinent facts
relating to it to the Attorney General's Office and the San Bernardino County District Attorney's
Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from
either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) Furthermore, we are required to advise you that, for purposes
of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any
individual other than the requestor." (Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTIONS

(1) Does the Act prohibit Rim of the World Park District Board Member Bialecki from
taking part in governmental decisions relating to a contract between the Park District and the Rim
of the World Trails Alliance ("ROWTA"), an inchoate nonprofit organization pursuing tax-exempt
status under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(3) given that he is also an alternate board
member of ROWTA?

(2) Does Section 1090 prohibit Board Member Bialecki from making or participating in the
making of, or the Park District Board from entering into, a contract with ROWTA to collaborate on

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-207
Page No. 2

a trail system, provide financial support to ROWTA, and allow ROWTA free use of the Park
District's Community Center for ROWTA meetings?

CONCLUSIONS

(1) The Act is not implicated by the questions presented. However, as stated below Section
1090 requires Board Member Bialecki to recuse himself from the decisions.

(2) Because the remote interest of Section 1091(b)(1) applies, Board Member Bialecki must
disclose his interest in the potential contract between the Park District and ROWTA and recuse
himself from decisions relating to the contract. However, the Park District is not prohibited from
entering into the contract.²

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You are the General Counsel of the Rim of the World Park District and the authorized
representative of Board Member Hugh Bialecki. Board Member Bialecki is also an alternate board
member for ROWTA, a nonprofit public benefit corporation organized under the California
Nonprofit Public Corporation Law (Corporations Code Section 5110, et seq.) for charitable and
public purposes. ROWTA is currently in the process of pursuing tax-exempt status under Internal
Revenue Code Section 501(c)(3).

ROWTA has requested that the Park District place a resolution on its agenda that would
constitute a contract between the Park District and ROWTA requiring the Park District and
ROWTA to collaborate on a trail system, the Park District to provide financial support to ROWTA,
and the Park District to allow ROWTA free use of the Park District Community Center for
ROWTA meetings. You state that the Park District has yet to consider the resolution.

On October 15, 2019, you stated that Board Member Bialecki does not receive any
compensation for his service as an alternate board member for ROWTA. On November 14, 2019,
you stated that ROWTA currently is not pursuing tax exempt status under state law.

ANALYSIS

The Act

The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from taking part in a
governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision would have a material
financial effect on one or more of the official's financial interests. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) An
official's interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act are
identified in Section 87103 and include interest in certain sources of income and an interest in the

² While you have indicated the Park District and ROWTA will collaborate on a trail system and the Park
District may provide financial support to ROWTA, you have not sought advice regarding the question of whether
ROWTA is a governmental agency and we do not have the facts necessary to make this determination at this time.
Accordingly, we express no opinion as to whether ROWTA is a governmental entity for purposes of the Act, but we do
note that the conclusions reached in this response do not apply to the extent that ROWTA is a governmental entity. If
you need assistance determining whether ROWTA is a governmental entity, you should seek additional advice.

File No. A-19-207
Page No. 3

official's personal finances. However, the facts provided indicate that Board Member Bialecki does
not receive income from ROWTA, and there is no indication that a contract between the Park
District and ROWTA will implicate Board Member Bialecki's personal finances. Accordingly, the
Act's conflict of interest provisions are not implicated by the questions presented.

Section 1090

Section 1090 generally prohibits a public officer or employee, while acting in his or her
official capacity, from making or participating in the making of a contract in which the he or she is
financially interested. The California Supreme Court has stated that the purpose of Section 1090 is
to make certain that "every public officer be guided solely by the public interest, rather than by
personal interest, when dealing with contracts in an official capacity." (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38
Cal.3d 633, 650.) Section 1090 is intended "not only to strike at actual impropriety, but also to
strike at the appearance of impropriety." (City of Imperial Beach v. Bailey (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d
191, 197.) A contract that violates Section 1090 is void. (Thomson, supra, at p. 646.)

Under Section 1090, a member of a public agency's governing body is conclusively
presumed to participate in the making of a contract under the governing body's authority,
irrespective of whether he or she actually participates in the making of that contract. (Id. at pp. 649-
650.) Therefore, Section 1090 prohibits the entire body from entering into a contract in which a
member of the body is financially interested, even if that member abstains from participating in the
making of, or fully discloses his or her financial interest in, the contract. (Ibid.)

Board Member Bialecki is subject to Section 1090 as an officer of the Park District, and the
proposed Rim of the World Park District resolution would constitute a contract between the Park
District and ROWTA. Because any Park District Board Member is presumed to participate in the
making of a Park District contract, Section 1090 prohibits any financially interested Board Member
from making or participating in the making of, and the Park District from entering into, the contract
with ROWTA unless a statutory exception or the rule of necessity applies.

As pertinent to the questions posed, Section 1091.5(a) provides that a public officer or
employee has a noninterest in the contract at issue if the interest is:

"(8) That of a noncompensated officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation,
which, as one of its primary purposes supports the functions of the body or board or
to which the body or board has a legal obligation to give particular consideration, and
provided further that this interest is noted in its official records.

"For purposes of this paragraph, an officer is "noncompensated" even though he
or she receives reimbursement from the nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation for
necessary travel and other actual expenses incurred in performing the duties of his or
her office.

[P]...[P]

"(12) That of (A) a bona fide nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation having among its
primary purposes the conservation, preservation, or restoration of park and natural

File No. A-19-207
Page No. 4

lands or historical resources for public benefit, which corporation enters into an
agreement with a public agency to provide services related to park and natural lands
or historical resources and which services are found by the public agency, prior to
entering into the agreement or as part of the agreement, to be necessary to the public
interest to plan for, acquire, protect, conserve, improve, or restore park and natural
lands or historical resources for public purposes and (B) any officer, director, or
employee acting pursuant to the agreement on behalf of the nonprofit corporation."

The noninterest exceptions of both Section 1091.5(a)(8) and (12) apply to a public officer or
employee's interest in a "tax-exempt" nonprofit corporation. While you have stated that ROWTA
operating as a nonprofit public benefit corporation organized under the state's Nonprofit Public
Benefit Corporation Law, it has not at this time obtained "tax-exempt" status. Because ROWTA has not yet
been approved for tax-exempt status, under either the Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(3) or
state law, the noninterest exceptions of Section 1091.5(a)(8) and (12) do not apply.³

Turning to the remote interest exceptions provided under Section 1091(a), "[a]n officer shall
not be deemed to be interested in a contract entered into by a body or board of which the officer is a
member within the meaning of this article if the officer has only a remote interest in the contract
and if the fact of that interest is disclosed to the body or board of which the officer is a member and
noted in its official records, and thereafter the body or board authorizes, approves, or ratifies the
contract in good faith by a vote of its membership sufficient for the purpose without counting the
vote or votes of the officer or member with the remote interest." As pertinent to the questions
posed, Section 1091(b)(1) provides that a public officer or employee has a remote interest if his or
her interest is:

"(1) That of an officer or employee of a nonprofit entity exempt from taxation
pursuant to Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (26 U.S.C. Sec.
501(c)(3)), pursuant to Section 501(c)(5) of the Internal Revenue Code (26 U.S.C.
Sec. 501(c)(5)), or a nonprofit corporation, except as provided in paragraph (8) of
subdivision (a) of Section 1091.5."

Unlike the noninterest exceptions in Section 1091.5(a)(8) and (12), Section 1091(b)(1)'s
remote interest exception does not require "tax exempt" status in applying to any "nonprofit
corporation, except as provided in paragraph 8 of subdivision (a) of Section 1091.5." Provided that
ROWTA is operating as a nonprofit public benefit corporation organized under the state's
Nonprofit Public Corporation Law as you have stated, the remote interest exception in Section
1091(b)(1) applies with respect to the potential contract between the Park District and ROWTA.
Barring additional facts, the Park District is not prohibited from entering into that potential contract,
but Board Member Bialecki must disclose his interest in the potential contract between the Park
District and ROWTA and recuse himself from decisions relating to that potential contract.

Note, however, that this conclusion is based upon the facts provided and most significantly
that ROWTA is operating as a nonprofit public benefit corporation organized under the state's
Nonprofit Public Corporation Law. As stated above, the Commission is not the finder of facts and is

³ We note, however, that if ROWTA achieves tax-exempt status under Internal Revenue Code Section
501(c)(3) or state law, the noninterest exceptions of Section 1091.5(a)(8) and (12) would subsequently apply.

File No. A-19-207
Page No. 5

not authorized to provide advice on bodies of law outside of the Act and Section 1090. Ultimately
Board Member Bialecki is responsible for determining whether ROWTA is fulfilling the
requirements of the Nonprofit Public Corporation Law based on his knowledge of ROWTA as an
alternative board member. Accordingly, Board Member Bialecki may wish to pursue assistance
from private counsel regarding nonprofit laws, which we cannot provide.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

BGL:aja

QUESTION: (1) Does the Act prohibit Rim of the World Park District Board Member Bialecki from 
taking part in governmental decisions relating to a contract between the Park District and the Rim 
of the World Trails Alliance ("ROWTA"), an inchoate nonprofit organization pursuing tax-exempt 
status under Internal Revenue Code Section 50l(c)(3) given that he is also an alternate board 
member of ROWTA? 
(2) Does Section 1090 prohibit Board Member Bial~cki from making or participating in the 
making of, or the Park District Board from entering into, a contract with ROWTA to collaborate ona trail system, provide financial support to ROWTA, and allow ROWTA free use of the Park 
District's Community Center for ROWTA meetings?

CONCLUSION: (1) The Act is not implicated by the questions presented. However, as stated below Section 
1090 requires Board Member Bialecki to recuse himself from the decisions. 
(2) Because the remote interest of Section 1091 (b )(1) applies, Board Member Bialecki must 
disclose his interest in the potential contract between the Park District and ROWTA and recuse 
himself from decisions relating to the contract. However, the Park District is not prohibited from 
entering into the contract. 2
